questions.text: Which of the following is a Lewis base?
questions.explanation: Ammonia has a lone pair of electrons available to donate, fitting the Lewis definition of a base.

questions.text: What is the pH of a 0.01 M NaOH solution?
Which of the following is the conjugate base of H2PO4-?
questions.explanation: A conjugate base is formed when the acid (H2PO4-) loses one proton (H+).

questions.text: A buffer solution is prepared by mixing:
questions.explanation: A buffer requires a weak acid/base and its corresponding salt (conjugate pair).

questions.text: The pH at the equivalence point of a titration between a strong acid and a weak base is:
questions.explanation: The salt produced undergoes hydrolysis to form an acidic solution.

questions.text: In a Galvanic cell, the electrode where oxidation occurs is the:
questions.explanation: Oxidation always occurs at the anode electrode.

questions.text: During the electrolysis of molten NaCl, which product is formed at the cathode?
questions.explanation: Sodium ions (Na+) are reduced to sodium metal (Na) at the cathode.

questions.text: Which of the following is used as an electrolyte in a dry cell (Leclanché cell)?
questions.explanation: Ammonium chloride serves as the electrolyte in traditional dry cells.

questions.text: The main raw material for the manufacture of sulfuric acid by the Contact Process is:
questions.explanation: Sulfur or iron pyrites are burned to produce the starting SO2 gas.

questions.text: Which catalyst is used in the Haber Process for the synthesis of ammonia?
questions.explanation: Finely divided iron with molybdenum as a promoter is used in ammonia synthesis.

questions.text: Which of the following is a nitrogenous fertilizer?
questions.explanation: Ammonium sulfate contains nitrogen, an essential plant nutrient.

questions.text: Which of the following is a thermoplastic polymer?
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics like polyethylene soften when heated and harden when cooled, allowing them to be reshaped.

questions.text: Which of the following polymers is a polyamide?
questions.explanation: Nylon-6,6 is a polyamide formed by the condensation reaction between a diamine and a dicarboxylic acid.

questions.text: The monomer of natural rubber is:
questions.explanation: Natural rubber is a polymer of isoprene (2-methyl-1,3-butadiene).

questions.text: Which of the following is a synthetic fiber?
questions.explanation: Dacron (a polyester) is a synthetic fiber, whereas the others are natural fibers.

questions.text: Which polymer is formed by condensation polymerization?
questions.explanation: Condensation polymerization involves the elimination of small molecules like water during the formation of the polymer.

questions.text: Vulcanization of rubber involves heating it with:
questions.explanation: Sulfur creates cross-links between polymer chains, increasing the durability and elasticity of the rubber.

questions.text: Which of the following is an addition polymer?
questions.explanation: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is formed by the repeated addition of vinyl chloride monomers without the loss of any small molecules.

questions.text: The repeating unit in Teflon is:
questions.explanation: Teflon (polytetrafluoroethylene) is made from the monomer tetrafluoroethene.

questions.text: The functional group of an ester is:
questions.explanation: Esters are derivatives of carboxylic acids where the hydrogen of the hydroxyl group is replaced by an alkyl group (R).

questions.text: The IUPAC name for the compound CH3-CH2-CH(OH)-CH3 is:
questions.explanation: The longest carbon chain has four carbons, and the hydroxyl group is on the second carbon[cite: 1].

questions.text: The reaction between an alcohol and a carboxylic acid to form an ester is called:
questions.explanation: Esterification is a condensation reaction that specifically produces an ester and water[cite: 1].

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct name for CH3CH2OCH2CH3?
questions.explanation: Diethyl ether is the common name, while ethoxyethane is the systematic IUPAC name[cite: 1].

questions.text: Which of the following compounds is a secondary alcohol?
questions.explanation: In isopropyl alcohol, the carbon attached to the -OH group is bonded to two other carbon atoms[cite: 1].

questions.text: The oxidation of a primary alcohol using a strong oxidizing agent like KMnO4 yields a(n):
questions.explanation: Primary alcohols oxidize first to aldehydes and then further to carboxylic acids under strong conditions[cite: 1].

questions.text: Which of the following is the general formula for an aldehyde?
questions.explanation: Aldehydes contain a carbonyl group bonded to at least one hydrogen atom at the end of a carbon chain[cite: 1].

questions.text: The product of the reaction between an organic acid and a base is a(n):
questions.explanation: Like inorganic acids, organic acids (carboxylic acids) undergo neutralization with bases to form salts and water[cite: 1].
